name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface

summary.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
summary.hypothesis: Round 39 replayed the bounded PTX wait/commit-window reopen and regressed from 24.17151993 ms to 24.20684814 ms even though the headline scheduler counters stayed broadly flat. That is enough negative evidence to stop spending another immediate round on the same micro-retime family. The highest-value move for round 40 is therefore to restore the exact accepted `489574e` PTX surface again, re-anchor the search on the clean plateau, and only then decide whether the next post-checkpoint branch should reopen a different family or escalate back to a structural pivot.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Source drift away from the accepted PTX hot-band steady state after a measured-loss reopen, not DRAM saturation and not an immediate need for another same-family micro-retime.
summary.risk: Low. The exact restore surface is already buildable and previously measured as the accepted best, so this is a bounded recovery action rather than a novel correctness risk.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus 24.20684814 ms and the accepted 24.16427231 ms anchor
ranking_notes: The round-39 PTX reopen closed as a clean regression, so the loop should re-anchor before taking another branch.
Restoring the exact `489574e` surface remains the fastest path back to the accepted plateau before the 5-round checkpoint.
The restore family is still the highest-confidence dispatch whenever the active PTX branch drifts or a reopen closes as a loss.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the exact restore no longer reproduces the expected plateau or if the restored source unexpectedly fails build/correctness on the current tree.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
